title: Real A/B: same code-navigation task, with vs without Axil, at equal correctness
method: For each task, a grep/read-only agent and an Axil-CLI-only agent (both Opus 4.8, general-purpose) answer the same where/how question in byte-identical sandboxes; a judge scores each; only tasks BOTH answer correctly are counted. Tokens recomputed mechanically by re-executing consulted artifacts (scripts/context-ab-score.py). Steps = consulted tool roundtrips (~agent turns).
index: axil index (structural proxies, top-level symbols); no SCIP
notes: Two rounds merged (12/12/13 tasks; 29 counted both-correct). Token reduction scales with repo size: flask(tiny) 41.8%, fastapi(mid) 76.3%, django(large) 77.7%. Steps fewer everywhere (9-32%). Sample-size lesson: flask read -20% at n=3, +50% at n=7, +42% at n=11 - always quote n. Aggregates include per-task losses (fastapi APIRouter, django SQLCompiler.as_sql) - measured, not cherry-picked.
